data.narrative:
Planet Labs is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company founded by Boshuizen, Marshall, and Schingler. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 3 same-sector public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M). The peer set reflects publicly traded frontier-tech operators at equivalent development stages.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the three comps is $6.3B, with a stated range spanning $900M to $37.0B. This method anchors the analysis directly to observable public market pricing of functionally similar businesses. A revenue-multiple approach using the sector's public P/S median of 59.8x could not be applied, as Planet Labs' reported revenue is unavailable in this dataset. The company's stated current valuation of $7B+ aligns near the upper bound of the peer-derived range.

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $5.4B to $7.2B, centered on a median of $6.3B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—a 41x spread from floor to ceiling—and the limited applicability of revenue multiples. The peer-median approach remains the most defensible method given available data, though the heterogeneity of the Space & Aerospace sector and Planet Labs' specific business model warrant careful judgment around placement within the derived range.
